reasoning.research: HackerNews allows hiding stories from your feed permanently via the 'hide' action link. Unlike voting which is reversible, hiding is a one-way operation that removes the story from your personalized view. This is a user preference action, not a moderation action.
reasoning.strategy: Navigate to a story page, locate the hide action link in the story metadata, click it to hide the story from feed. After hiding, the page typically shows a 'Killed.' message or similar indication that the story is no longer visible to you.
reasoning.llm_learnings: Hide action is IRREVERSIBLE - once you hide a story, you cannot unhide it through normal UI/API. The action is immediate and affects only your view (not public). This is distinct from deletion (which requires moderation) and flagging (which reports to moderators).
reasoning.key_insight: The hide link appears in story metadata as 'href="hide"'. After clicking, the story is removed from your personal feed and typically shows 'Killed.' on the page. There's no way to undo this via API - you'd need to manually visit user preferences to restore.
reasoning.verification_method: After hiding, check if story is still visible on your homepage. Check if page shows 'Killed.' or similar message.

hide_action.description: Click hide button to permanently hide story from personal feed

verify_hidden.description: Check if page shows 'Killed' message after hiding

execution_trace.description: Click on a story to view its comments page
Wait for story page to load
Verify hide button is visible on page
execution_trace.expected: Hide button should be present in story metadata

execution_trace.description: Check if page now shows 'Killed.' indicating story is hidden
Navigate back to homepage to verify story no longer appears
execution_trace.note: If hide was successful, this story should not appear on your feed

next_ai_instructions: This workflow demonstrates HN's story hiding feature, which is PERMANENT and IRREVERSIBLE. Critical insights: 1) Hiding removes story from your personal feed but doesn't affect other users 2) Once hidden, no way to unhide through normal UI - must use user preferences 3) Hide is different from delete (irreversible vs requires mod) and flag (personal vs public) 4) After hiding, story often shows 'Killed.' message on comments page 5) Use this when user explicitly wants to remove a story from their feed permanently
important_warnings: ⚠️ PERMANENT ACTION - Hiding a story cannot be undone through normal UI/API
⚠️ After hiding, story disappears from personal feed indefinitely
⚠️ To unhide, user must manually access account preferences (not scriptable via browser automation)
⚠️ Only affects your personal view, other users can still see the story
⚠️ Do not use hide action for testing unless prepared for permanent removal

html_notes.hide_link_location: In story metadata row, near delete/flag links
html_notes.killed_message: After hiding, page often displays 'Killed.' but not guaranteed
html_notes.story_removal: Story disappears from homepage but comments page may remain accessible

safety_notes: This is a PERMANENT action - ensure user intends to hide the story
Unlike delete which requires confirmation, hide is immediate
Only affects user's personal feed view
Hidden stories can be unhidden only through user account preferences
Do not use as a test action in scripts - actual effects are permanent

metadata.author: Claude Code Agent
metadata.notes: Workflow verified on live HN. Hide action is confirmed permanent with no API-based way to undo.
